channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger's defense claims he has autism in bid to avoid firing squad
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's autism diagnosis should take the death penalty off the table if he is convicted of the Idaho student murders, defense lawyer argues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE deports Dominican migrant wanted for human smuggling that led to death of 3 minors
item.description: ICE deported Batista Matos, 36, to his home country of the Dominican Republic for the fifth time on Monday to face charges in connection with fatal human smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump has dropped a high-profile abortion case in Idaho. Here's what that means
item.description: A yearslong legal battle over the right to an emergency abortion in Idaho has been abruptly upended now that President Donald Trump has moved to drop the high-profile case.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi And Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House hunting surges where LA wildfires raged: Report
item.description: As Los Angeles residents recover from wildfires, house-hunting in many of the communities affected by the fires is reportedly surging.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Veterans fired from federal jobs say they feel betrayed, including some who voted for Trump
item.description: Veterans who have been abruptly fired from federal jobs say they feel betrayed by the Trump administration's dramatic downsizing of government.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Businesses scramble to contain fallout from Trump's tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico
item.description: A Minnesota farmer worries about the price of fertilizer. A San Diego entrepreneur deals with an unexpected cost increase of remodeling a restaurant. A Midwestern sheet metal fabricator bemoans the prospect of higher aluminum prices.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy even went public on its website with a statement regarding the buildings, saying it understands their configuration is a "sensitive topic.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams faces calls to resign from Democrats at fiery House hearing on immigration
item.description: Embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) faced a barrage of criticism at a House Oversight and Accountability Committee hearing with so-called sanctuary city mayors on Wednesday — with much of the backlash coming from his fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans need to cut Medicaid to hit budget savings target, CBO finds
item.description: Republicans can’t achieve their goal of slashing $2 trillion in federal spending over the next decade without cutting Medicaid, according to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office (CBO).
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles County sues Southern California Edison, alleging utility's equipment sparked wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Los Angeles County said Wednesday that it's suing Southern California Edison, alleging the utility’s equipment sparked January’s Eaton Fire, which destroyed more than 9,400 structures and killed 17 people in the Altadena area.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: By Jake Offenhartz And Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US travel advisory for Turks and Caicos Islands updated, year after Americans arrested
item.description: The updated advisory comes nearly a year after several Americans were arrested on ammunition charges in Turks and Caicos.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KRON4

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes gym membership tax credit
item.description: “They can get a move away from the pharmaceuticals and focus a little bit more on just lifestyle changes,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Trump could reduce Mexico, Canada tariffs, commerce secretary says
item.description: "Maybe — he’ll consider giving them relief until we get to, of course, April 2," the Commerce Secretary told Bloomberg.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Fired Los Angeles Fire Chief Kristin Crowley has a new job
item.description: Crowley, the first woman to serve as LAFD's Chief, was fired by Mayor Karen Bass on Feb. 21 after almost three years in the role.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 31 million Americans borrowed money for health care last year: Poll
item.description: (The Hill) — More than 31 million Americans borrowed money last year to pay for health care, a new survey found. Those Americans borrowed about $74 billion, despite most of them have some form of health insurance, the West Health-Gallup survey found.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge tells attorneys to stop being so secretive in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho judge is warning attorneys to quit filing so many sealed documents in the murder case of a man accused in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sanctuary city mayors, including Chicago's Brandon Johnson, to face Congress amid immigration tensions
item.description: Mayors of Chicago, NYC, Boston and Denver are preparing to defend their cities' sanctuary status, a policy that has faced increased security under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, WGN 9

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: Companies that depend on cross-border trade were already facing higher prices in anticipation of the 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada that took effect Tuesday. Now disruption looms, and if consumers won’t accept steep price hikes, they’ll have to downsize operations and lay off workers.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US tariffs on Canada, Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Ariana Grande goes full Glinda, 'Wicked' bestie Erivo does Elphaba proud in green on Oscars carpet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ariana Grande did her Glinda proud in a pale pink stunner, and her “Wicked” bestie Cynthia Erivo honored Elphaba in deep forest green on the Oscars carpet as high glam took over Sunday. Others went for bold red, classic black and pops of pink.
item.dc:creator: LEANNE ITALIE and BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to watch when Trump gives his big speech to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump loves a good spectacle, and it's hard to top a speech to a joint session of Congress. The House chamber is packed with lawmakers, and the president's arrival is announced in a booming voice by the sergeant-at-arms, triggering cacophonous applause.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Foul! Cavaliers guard Ty Jerome blasts officials after whistle-happy crew calls 53 fouls
item.description: Upset with an inordinate number of personals called during Cleveland's 133-129 overtime win against Portland on Sunday, Jerome didn't hold back in criticizing the officiating crew — zeroing in on referee Natalie Sago.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5
